cards.answer: This aggregate EvoLOCUS artifact summarizes 1,517,672 laws across 50 states from 1,000 units (823 cities, 177 counties). Ontology coverage includes 1,018 nodes and 3,000 edges, with top elements Rules (962,831) and Other (407,704). Raw ordinance text is not published. Model-produced scores remain neutral unless direction is verified. Dataset revision: main; analysis state: local_aggregate_ready.
cards.detail: Open the high-level answer before drilling into unit or evidence views.
cards.label: Grok summary
cards.question: What does the current real aggregate layer cover?

route.question: What does the real-data Pages layer currently cover?

cards.title: Offline aggregate synthesis

cards.answer: This question pack includes 8 prompts (dataset revision main) titled Current view, CA focus, Other topic, Rules function, Tier 1 tier, Audit signals, Selected unit, and Local package. Apply filters to select relevant prompts for interface queries. GitHub Pages does not make live model calls. Raw ordinance text and source locators are not published.
cards.detail: Use the generated question to color the map before opening the answer.

cards.answer: The map supports aggregate color modes for neutral tier, dominant topic, dominant function, and law-count intensity. Colors are exploratory model-output summaries, not rankings.

cards.answer: The current ontology has 1018 nodes and 3000 edges linking jurisdiction units to released topics, functions, neutral tiers, and score dimensions.

cards.answer: los_angeles_county is the highest-count published aggregate unit in this artifact, with 11503 rows. This is a navigation route, not a ranking or legal conclusion.

citation: Denis Peskoff, Joe Barrow, Christopher Vu, and Diag Davenport. "Freeing the Law with LOCUS: A Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States." arXiv:2606.19334, 2026.

grok.browser_policy: Model calls run only in local tooling or GitHub Actions. Pages receives sanitized aggregate JSON.

publication_policy.notice: Cards summarize aggregate artifacts for research review. They are not legal advice or legal findings.
